description_long: This is a compact, single-rotor helicopter with a streamlined fuselage painted in a desert camouflage pattern, featuring tan, brown, and beige tones. The helicopter has a rounded nose with a large, dark-tinted cockpit window that wraps around the front, providing wide visibility for the pilot and co-pilot. The main rotor consists of two long, slender blades mounted atop a central mast, while the tail boom extends rearward to a small vertical stabilizer and a two-blade tail rotor. The landing gear consists of two parallel metal skids connected by crossbars, designed for stable ground contact. The body appears to be constructed primarily from lightweight metal alloys, with reinforced areas around the cockpit and engine housing. No visible registration numbers or markings are present on the exterior.
description: A small, desert-camouflaged helicopter with a rounded cockpit, two-blade main and tail rotors, and metal landing skids. The cockpit features a large, dark-tinted window wrapping around the front.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, camouflage paint, main and tail rotors, and both landing skids.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rounded, dark-tinted cockpit window and the symmetrical arrangement of the landing skids.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail boom, camouflage pattern, and the two-blade tail rotor.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail boom, vertical stabilizer, and the tail rotor configuration.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, narrow, straight main rotor blades made of composite or metal, colored black with no visible markings, each blade approximately 4 meters in length.
attribute_tail_rotor: Two-blade tail rotor mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, blades are black and appear to be made of composite or metal, with no visible markings or protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel, tubular metal landing skids painted in a light color, connected by two crossbars, providing stable support for the helicopter on the ground.
attribute_cockpit_windows: One large, curved, dark-tinted cockpit window wrapping around the front and sides, framed by thin metal supports, providing wide visibility; no visible markings.
